pi_items.verified_value: File exists at examples/equest_env/data/4StoreyBuilding.inp; path reflects Windows container environment path
pi_items.source: File system inspection: 93KB .inp file contains 3,258 lines of valid BDL (Building Description Language) code

pi_items.verified_value: Primary CHW Loop - standard naming convention for primary chilled water circulation loops
pi_items.source: BDL naming conventions and HVAC system design standards

pi_items.verified_value: Main Air-Cooled Chiller - valid descriptive name for CHILLER object in BDL
pi_items.source: eQUEST/BDL documentation: CHILLER is a valid object type in DOE-2.2 schema

pi_items.verified_value: 1.05 kW/ton is reasonable for air-cooled chillers; converts to EIR of 0.29856 (verified via conversion formula)
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed: air-cooled chillers typically range 0.8-1.2 kW/ton; federal FEMP standards target 0.544 kW/ton, so 1.05 is mid-range; conversion formula: EIR = (kW/ton × 3412.14) / 12000 = (1.05 × 3412.14) / 12000 = 0.29856

pi_items.verified_value: 44.0°F is the industry-standard chilled water supply setpoint temperature
pi_items.source: Web search confirmed: ARI (Air-Conditioning, Heating, and Refrigeration Institute) standard specifies 44°F supply leaving chiller, 54°F return (10°F ΔT) at full load conditions

pi_items.verified_value: eQUEST v3.65 is a legitimate free building energy simulation tool; runs on Windows only; integrates DOE-2 engine; build 7175 released October 4, 2018
pi_items.source: Web search verified: https://www.doe2.com/equest/ and https://energy-models.com/software/equest confirm eQUEST 3.65 specifications

pi_items.metadata_value: Task uses BDL (Building Description Language) for verification
pi_items.verified_value: BDL is the correct format for DOE-2/eQUEST; verified objects: CIRCULATION-LOOP type CHILLED-WATER, CHILLER type ELEC-AIR-COOLED, COOL-SET-T property, COOLING-EIR parameter all valid
pi_items.source: DOE-2.2 documentation and eQUEST user guides confirm BDL syntax and object types

pi_items.metadata_value: 4StoreyBuilding contains existing HVAC system to extend
pi_items.verified_value: Baseline file contains one existing CIRCULATION-LOOP: 'DHW Plant 1 Loop (1)' (hot water); no existing chilled water loops or chillers; task requires agent to ADD new objects
pi_items.source: Content inspection via grep: baseline has 0 instances of 'Primary CHW Loop' or 'Main Air-Cooled Chiller'; 1 CIRCULATION-LOOP exists (DHW)

pi_items.verified_value: Reasonable - task requires: (1) GUI navigation in eQUEST, (2) understanding of Water-Side HVAC module, (3) creating two interconnected objects with specific parameters, (4) saving file; no equivalent objects in baseline to modify
pi_items.source: Task structure analysis and baseline model assessment

pi_items.verified_value: Mathematically correct: (1.05 kW/ton × 3412.14 Btu/h per kW) / 12000 Btu/h per ton = 0.298562; tolerance ±0.005 = acceptable range [0.29356, 0.30356]
pi_items.source: Direct calculation verification; confirmed against eQUEST documentation on EIR conversion methodology

privileged_info_summary: Central Plant Chiller Setup is a GUI automation task for eQUEST 3.65, a legitimate free Windows-based building energy simulation tool using DOE-2 engine. The task requires creating a chilled water circulation loop named 'Primary CHW Loop' with 44°F supply setpoint (industry standard) and an air-cooled chiller named 'Main Air-Cooled Chiller' with 1.05 kW/ton efficiency (equivalent to 0.29856 EIR), attached to the loop. The baseline 4StoreyBuilding is a 3,258-line synthetic building model with no existing chilled water infrastructure. Verifier correctly parses BDL format, validates object types (CIRCULATION-LOOP type CHILLED-WATER, CHILLER type ELEC-AIR-COOLED), and checks efficiency via COOLING-EIR parameter using mathematically correct conversion. All metadata claims have been independently verified against authoritative sources.
